channel.title: Comments for page "Factored Obsolescence"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Factored Obsolescence"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I'm… frustrated with this piece, mostly because of the context it exists in.

+ show block
– hide block

Like, if this were a tale on its own, it's pretty brutal and the matchup is way out of left field, but by the end I'd probably have upvoted it based on interest in the follow-up. The circumstances by the end are pretty cool, and I want to see where this goes. It also feels like an interesting way to get into (what I assume is) Ad Astra.

But it's not in a vacuum. Taken as a single OCT entry, it at least partially feels like brutal torture directed at random. Knowing that your next tales in the contest are going to involve ToasterHead, well… The worst case scenario would be a continuation of this setting but is mostly just vignettes of other characters being tortured (I'm not saying you'll do this, you seem to have plans, but that would be the worst). Even the best case scenario I could imagine - say some kind of complex space opera/Alien-style horror thing involving ToasterHead where the other characters get involved in interesting ways - still doesn't particularly interest me. Given that the one action piece of the current story is one character doing something to the utmost of their ability and ToasterHead still "winning" and the torture continuing… if that's core to the experience, that bodes of a slasher-movie-style horror story I don't want to read.

No vote I guess.


I'll keep an eye out for the next tales, but I can't get excited for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was a little taken aback by how much harsher the treatment of Sinclair is here than in Ihp's version of events

Yeah, I… kinda got lost in the moment while writing this one. I tried to capture some of the original 2856 in it, and think I at least somewhat succeeded there.

I don't think it's a sustainable storytelling format for a 5-round competition.

Well, consider this:

  • I've set the scene as the far future, where things are different and other, unknown forces might be at play.
  • I've placed a normally headstrong character in a position where they are physically and emotionally weak, neither of which they will be recovering from any time soon.
  • I've established that the Farmhouse, and T.H. in particular, have been supplied with only comotose patients for at least a century.

And yeah, long-form storytelling is definitely not my strong suit, but hopefully I've created enough of a setting/character selection that it'll work as the basis for something longer :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really loved the prose in this one! The sense of peril and urgency was conveyed very well. I was a little taken aback by how much harsher the treatment of Sinclair is here than in Ihp's version of events, but I suppose that makes sense, given that it's coming from the other side of the competition.

I appreciated that she still got a couple of opportunities for badassery, but by the end of this, I couldn't help feeling like this wasn't so much a story as it was a series of traumatic events. That may match the horror and sadism of 2856, but I don't think it's a sustainable storytelling format for a 5-round competition. If you advance from here, I hope you'll consider showing us more facets of Toasterhead's personality in future interactions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Woo, contest time! Thanks to GrimmCreeper, UncleNicolini, WineDarkSea, weryllium and 9Volt for all giving this a look over, and my apologies to Ihp for traumatising and torturing his character.
